name: The Documentation Brec Can't Deliver
description: Brec has eight months of data showing the Lazarus subjects' crystal resonance shifted simultaneously on the night the Grid reactivated. He can't reach Stone through official channels. He needs an unofficial carrier.
accept_text: He hands you the folder with the same care he uses handling the monitoring equipment — precise, without wasted motion. 'Eight months of daily readings,' he says. 'The full comparison chart is on top, the raw data behind it, and my analysis is at the back with my methodology so she can check whether I've read it correctly.' He pauses. 'The analysis is: on the night eight months ago, every subject in this chamber simultaneously shifted resonance frequency in the same direction. Not a cascade — a single simultaneous event, affecting complete-crystal subjects and early-stage subjects the same way, regardless of integration level.' He looks at the folder. 'I believe this correlates with something that happened in the Grid infrastructure that night. I don't have clearance to access Grid resonance data to confirm the correlation. Director Stone does — or she can get it.' He looks at you. 'Tell her it's from Brec. She'll know who that is. Tell her I've been continuing the monitoring protocols she established and that the protocols are producing data she needs to see.' He pauses. 'And tell her — separately — that three months ago I got a communication from someone claiming to be Ministry of Grid Infrastructure who asked me to stop filing the monitoring reports and discontinue the daily readings. I declined. The communication stopped coming.' He looks at you. 'She should know that too.'
complete_text: Stone takes the folder without speaking and reads it standing up, the way she reads things when she doesn't want to pause long enough to sit. You watch her face, which is already lined by the specific expenditure of someone who has been carrying twenty years of research, and watch something change in it. Not surprise — she is past the stage of surprise. Something more like: confirmation of a hypothesis that she had hoped was wrong. She reads through the raw data at the back, checking Brec's methodology against something she carries in her head. Then she closes the folder. 'The simultaneous shift,' she says. 'I noticed it from outside. I didn't have enough data to confirm it was simultaneous rather than sequential.' She sets the folder down. 'Brec is right about the correlation. Eight months ago — the night the Grid reactivated — something in the lattice pulse affected the compound in all subjects simultaneously. The compound has been responding to the lattice ever since, in the direction the lattice is moving.' She pauses. 'Which means the compound I designed, twenty years ago, is responding to the Grid's construction wave. The wave that builds the network is pulling the compound's crystal component in its direction.' She is quiet. 'Which means the subjects' integration is moving. Not just growing — moving. Toward something.' She looks at the folder. 'Tell Brec to keep filing the daily readings. Tell him to send them to me through the maintenance delivery route — there's a junction at level four that accepts packages with no manifest check. He'll know where it is.' She pauses. 'And the Ministry communication telling him to stop.' Her expression is careful and precise and does not show what it is doing beneath that. 'That is useful information. That I will handle.'
